How This Course Is Organized

Module Progress To make it easy for you to go through the course the first time and then come back later and find exactly what
0/2 Lessons
0% you’re looking for, Idea Power is presented in four Modules .
Last activity on 24
November 2023 Introduction – Where you are now. The overview stuff to get you ready to dive in.
Adventures – The core of the course. You’ll get most of the value going through each Adventure in order.
Bonuses – Extra lessons and examples. Including how to take what you’re learning here to make AI tools
2 Lessons even more powerful.
Appendix – A few extra worksheets you’ll find helpful after you’ve completed the Adventures.	How and Why Ideas Can Change
Your Life
Your Lessons Are Adventures	Course Structure - How to Navigate
Your Way Through the Idea Power
Adventures and Boxes
Each Idea Power lesson is an Adventure . We think Adventure is the most accurate term for what you’re about
to experience.
Each Adventure builds on the one before. Each one will guide you through easy and fun (and repeatable ) ways
to come up with new ideas on any subject.
Previous Lesson Next Module	Mark as Complete
I suggest you pick one of these two ways to go through this course.
Read or listen to the first Adventure. Then read the corresponding chapter in your Mindbook. Then complete
the exercises (Boxes) for that Adventure.
In other words, dive deep into each Adventure. Fill in all the Boxes. Get everything you can out of each
Adventure before moving on to the next.
or…
Read or listen to ALL the Adventures first. Do a quick scan of everything in Idea Power.
Then start again from Adventure #1. Read or listen to it again. Read the corresponding chapter in your
Mindbook. Complete the exercises (Boxes) for that Adventure.
In other words, first get the big picture of everything here for you. Then dive deep into each Adventure.
I don’t think either approach is better. It’s about what’s best for you and how you like to learn.
The Idea-Generating Boxes
Most of the Idea Power Adventures include 1 or 2 exercises. I call them “ Boxes “. You’ll see why as you go
through the course.
It’s very important to understand that the sole function of these idea-starter boxes is to help you.
This means there is no “right” or “wrong” way to use them. So relax and have fun. Remember that whatever they
inspire you to come up with is absolutely, 100% correct.
It may help to take each question in a box in two ways.
First, literally. For example, if the question suggests coming up with ideas that have to do with speed, think of
things that literally have to do with speed—such as doing something faster or slower.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 1 of 2 --

But then, let yourself go and think of things that may only indirectly have to do with speed—or even things you just
happen to associate with speed. The important thing is to come up with good ideas—and not worry about
getting the “right” answer to the question.
Of course, you don’t have to answer each and every question in the boxes provided (although it’s good practice
to at least try).
That’s all the introduction info you need. You’re ready to dive in.
Navigating the Site and Recording Your Progress
MODULES As you move through each Adventure (and Bonus and even these short Introduction pages), you can record your
progress by tapping the Mark As Complete button.
That button advances the Percentage Complete wheel on the Idea Power home page. Marking lessons
“ complete ” makes it easy to pick up where you left off when you return.
You can move Adventure to Adventure using the Next Lesson and Previous Lesson links.
Next Module will get you from the Introduction , to the Adventures , to the Idea Power Bonuses .
At the top of every page, you’ll find navigation breadcrumb links. They look something like this:
Idea Power will always be first. Tapping that brings you to the course home page.
The 2nd item is the Module. Tap that to see the lessons (or Adventures) in that section.
The 3rd item is your current lesson or Adventure. Tapping that does nothing because you’re already there
🐀򠠀
There are several very intuitive ways to move through the materials. You’ll figure out the best ones for you.
Tap Mark As Complete , and then Next Module to continue.
